audit_result.proof_trace: The recovered polynomial coefficients are not validated to have degree < FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB. While the encoding side (eip7594.c:94-96) asserts the upper half coefficients are zero, recover_cells computes a full FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_EXT_BLOB coefficient array via coset_ifft without verifying coefficients [FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB, FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_EXT_BLOB) equal zero, violating the stated post-condition and erasure coding assumption.
audit_result.attack_scenario: An attacker crafts malicious cells that produce a recovered polynomial with non-zero coefficients in the upper half. When recover_cells_and_kzg_proofs recomputes proofs (eip7594.c:264), compute_fk20_cell_proofs expects only FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB coefficients but receives incorrect data, producing invalid proofs that could bypass downstream validation or cause state inconsistencies.

PROP-57888860-post-001.text: Recovered polynomial must have degree < FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB (truncation correctness).
PROP-57888860-post-001.assertion: forall recover(cell_indices, cells) -> poly: deg(poly) < FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB
